title: Redeeming or Transferring Bonds
body_text:
Redeeming or Transferring Bonds
1	Introduction to Redeeming or Transferring Bonds
1.1	Overview of Redeeming or Transferring Bonds
Central 1 is the agent for credit unions in redeeming or transferring bonds and Coupons.
The following types of bonds and Coupons are included in the procedures provided in this Part:
Canada Savings Bonds and Coupons
Ontario Savings Bonds
Government of Canada marketable bonds and Coupons
Treasury Bills
Important
:	Central 1 cannot process US Savings Bonds or send them on collection.
Requirements for preparing bonds for processing and instructions for completing forms are determined by government regulations and must be strictly adhered to. Errors in preparing bonds or Coupons for processing will result in either an adjustment to the credit unionâs Central 1 current account or the return of the bonds or Coupons to the credit union for correction.
1.2	Documentation Record Requirements
Certificate-style Canada Savings Bonds and Ontario Savings Bonds are processed through Branch Capture. It is, therefore, not necessary to make separate records (copies) of these items. Images are available on Branch Capture for 180 days and on ORS for 7 years. See the Deposit Processing Part for more information.
All other securities that are not
processed on Branch Capture must be scanned or photocopied to identify each item. No insurance claim is payable if the Insured has not recorded each item unless the record has been lost or destroyed or the item was not recorded due to circumstances beyond the control of the Insured. Credit unions send negotiable items to Central 1 as per the credit unionâs internal transportation policy by courier. See Section 3.2, Policy for Transporting Negotiable Items to Central 1.
